name: Yolo Object Detecion - for detecting a soft toy
short_description: Detecting Soft toys in frame usining fine-tuned yolo model
full_description: This component uses a fine-tuned YOLO (You Only Look Once) architecture for the specific task of detecting soft toys in image frames. It identifies whether a frame contains a soft toy and generates a bounding box around the detected object. The model is tailored for an ICICLE Year 4 demo use case, where camera traps act as motion-activated monitors. When triggered, they capture images and process them through this model to detect the presence of an object of interest. If detected, a notification is sent to a nearby edge device, which then deploys a drone to capture multidimensional video of the object.
keywords: wildlife, camera traps, object detection, yolo, conservation
author: swathivm

name: Ultralytics YOLO26n
short_description: YOLO26n.
full_description: YOLO26 is the latest evolution in the YOLO series of real-time object detectors, engineered from the ground up for edge and low-power devices. It introduces a streamlined design that removes unnecessary complexity while integrating targeted innovations to deliver faster, lighter, and more accessible deployment.
keywords: yolo, ultralytics, object detection
author: skhuvis

name: Ultralytics YOLO
short_description: YOLOv9
full_description: YOLOv9 marks a significant advancement in real-time object detection, introducing groundbreaking techniques such as Programmable Gradient Information (PGI) and the Generalized Efficient Layer Aggregation Network (GELAN). This model demonstrates remarkable improvements in efficiency, accuracy, and adaptability, setting new benchmarks on the MS COCO dataset. The YOLOv9 project, while developed by a separate open-source team, builds upon the robust codebase provided by UltralyticsYOLOv5, showcasing the collaborative spirit of the AI research community.
keywords: yolo, ultralytics, object detection
author: skhuvis
citation: YOLOv9: Learning What You Want to Learn Using Programmable Gradient Information by Wang, Chien-Yao and Liao, Hong-Yuan Mark (arXiv:2402.13616)

name: Segment Anything Model 3 (SAM 3)
short_description: Foundation model for promptable concept segmentation (PCS).
full_description: SAM 3 is a unified model that detects, segments, and tracks objects in images and videos based on concept prompts (text or image exemplars). It features a presence head that decouples recognition from localization, significantly boosting accuracy over previous SAM versions.
keywords: segmentation, zero-shot, computer vision, SAM3, tracking
author: Meta AI

name: OWLv2 Large Patch14 Ensemble
short_description: Ensemble version of OWLv2 for state-of-the-art zero-shot object detection.
full_description: OWLv2-Large-Patch14-Ensemble is an advanced iteration of the Open-World Localization model. It uses a CLIP-trained ViT-L/14 backbone and is scaled using the OWL-ST (Self-Training) recipe on over 1 billion image-text pairs. The ensemble variant is specifically optimized for high-confidence localization of rare and unseen object classes by combining multiple prediction heads or training checkpoints.
keywords: object detection, zero-shot, open-vocabulary, ensemble, google, computer vision
author: Google DeepMind

name: BioCLIP 2 (via pybioclip)
short_description: Biology foundation model for taxonomic classification and trait prediction.
full_description: BioCLIP 2 is a large-scale vision-language foundation model for biological understanding. It is trained on the TreeOfLife-200M dataset, which contains 214 million images across 952k taxa. Unlike standard CLIP, it utilizes hierarchical contrastive learning to align image representations with the taxonomic tree of life, allowing it to generalize to unseen species and even predict ecological traits like habitat and life stage.
keywords: biology, taxonomy, wildlife, organism detection, zero-shot, pybioclip, tree of life
author: John Bradley / Imageomics Institute

name: DINOv3 ViT-S/16 (LVD-1689M Pretrained)
short_description: Small-sized DINOv3 Vision Transformer (ViT-S/16) distilled from the 7B teacher.
full_description: DINOv3-ViTS16 is a self-supervised vision transformer distilled from the 7B parameter DINOv3 flagship model. It is trained on the LVD-1689M dataset using a composite objective of global DINO loss, patch-level iBOT loss, and Gram anchoring to preserve dense feature quality. This model produces highly reliable embeddings for both global classification tasks and dense visual tasks like segmentation and depth estimation without requiring labels.
keywords: self-supervised, vision transformer, distilled, backbone, feature extraction, meta
author: Meta AI
input_data: LVD-1689M (1.7B curated images from 17B Instagram pool)

name: SAM3 — segmentation-assisted detection
short_description: Segmentation-capable foundation model for prompt-driven localization; suitable for gated Hugging Face checkpoints when access is configured.
full_description: Provides a segmentation-oriented path for open-vocabulary or prompt-conditioned localization on images. Intended for workloads that rely on gated model weights through standard Hugging Face access workflows.
keywords: HVDSM3
author: haeparth

name: YOLOE

full_description: YOLO-style architecture branch for workloads that prioritize throughput while retaining open-set or configurable class behavior.
